argument_quality_notes.linguistic: Agent did not debate; provided no candidates.
argument_quality_notes.landscape: Good argumentation in debate, correctly identifying the hard constraint of LHT to eliminate right-hand traffic candidates.
argument_quality_notes.botanics: Good argumentation in debate, correctly identifying the hard constraint of LHT to eliminate right-hand traffic candidates.
argument_quality_notes.regulatory: Strong, consistent arguments citing hard constraints (LHT) to refute other agents.

revision_justification_notes.landscape: Revision was justified by the hard constraint of LHT provided by the regulatory agent.
revision_justification_notes.botanics: Revision was justified by the hard constraint of LHT provided by the regulatory agent.

debate_contribution_notes.landscape: Reiterated R1 points but framed them within the new LHT constraint.
debate_contribution_notes.botanics: Reiterated R1 points but framed them within the new LHT constraint.
debate_contribution_notes.regulatory: Reiterated R1 points but framed them within the new LHT constraint.

verdict.moderator_pairing_notes: The moderator correctly prioritized the Regulatory agent (hard constraints) against the disagreeing Landscape and Botanics agents. This is the optimal pairing strategy.
verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly followed the evidence hierarchy by accepting the Regulatory agent's LHT evidence to eliminate right-hand traffic countries. However, the final prediction (New Zealand) is incorrect (Ground Truth: France). The judge failed to identify that the visual evidence (LHT, white lines, tropical vegetation) is also consistent with France (specifically Réunion Island), and the agents failed to consider this possibility.
